name: vscode-agent-orchestrator
description: Graph-based orchestrator for multi-agent workflows in VS Code. Native chat participants per node, file-based handoff, GitHub-PR / timer / message triggers — works with any model the chat picker exposes.

commands.title: Agent Orchestrator: Open Node Chat Window
Agent Orchestrator: Emergency Stop (disable all triggers)

chatParticipants.name: orchestrator
chatParticipants.description: Run a node from your workflow graph. Use /run <node-id> ...

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run a node by id from the active workflow (e.g. /run sec)

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Resume the latest saved run for a node that reached the tool-round limit

commands.name: list
commands.description: List nodes defined in the active workflow

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.enabled.description: When false, all triggers are disabled.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.dryRun.description: When true, log dispatch intents to the ledger without making model calls.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.dispatchMode.description: How trigger and graph-editor node runs are dispatched. background runs independently in the extension host; chat uses the legacy VS Code chat panel path. Manual @orchestrator chat requests always run in chat.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.ghPollSeconds.description: How often to poll GitHub for new PRs/commits.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.toolRoundLimit.description: Maximum language-model tool-call rounds per orchestrated node run. Raise this for long implementation, PR, or MCP workflows. Repeated failed tool calls are stopped separately.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.maxToolsPerReque.description: Maximum number of tools to advertise to the model in a single request. VS Code currently rejects requests with more than 128 tools, so this cannot be unlimited.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.backgroundToolMo.description: Tool exposure policy for background extension-host runs. safe hides confirmation-heavy tools such as terminal commands and Monday.com mutations to avoid modal permission popups; all exposes every tool allowed by blockedTools and maxToolsPerRequest. Native chat dispatch still shows tool confirmations inline.

vscodeAgentOrchestrator.blockedTools.description: Registered language-model tool names or wildcard patterns to hide from orchestrated node runs. Set to [] to expose all native tools allowed by the active tool mode. Matching is case-insensitive and also handles namespaced names such as functions.manage_todo_list.

scripts.build: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview && npm run build:cli
scripts.typecheck: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p webview/tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.test.json
scripts.test: npm run typecheck && npm run test:unit
scripts.package:vsix: npm run build && vsce package --no-dependencies --out vscode-agent-orchestrator.vsix
